subject: humanities
name_zh: 美式 K-5 · 社会研究（C3）
description: C3 Framework: Civics, Economics, Geography, History. Inquiry arc: Developing questions, Applying disciplinary concepts, Evaluating sources, Communicating conclusions.

nodes.name: 规则与法律
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.3.K-2. Explain the need for and purposes of rules in various settings inside and outside of school.
D2.Civ.12.K-2. Identify and explain how rules function in public (classroom and school) settings.
D2.Civ.11.K-2. Explain how people can work together to make decisions in the classroom.

nodes.name: 政府与公民
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.1.K-2. Describe roles and responsibilities of people in authority.
D2.Civ.5.K-2. Explain what governments are and some of their functions.
D2.Civ.6.K-2. Describe how communities work to accomplish common tasks, establish responsibilities, and fulfill roles of authority.

nodes.name: 民主原则
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.8.K-2. Describe democratic principles such as equality, fairness, and respect for legitimate authority and rules.
D2.Civ.7.K-2. Apply civic virtues when participating in school settings.
D2.Civ.9.K-2. Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions while responding attentively to others when addressing ideas and making decisions as a group.

nodes.name: 权利与责任
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.2.K-2. Explain how all people, not just official leaders, play important roles in a community.
D2.Civ.6.K-2. Describe how communities work to accomplish common tasks, establish responsibilities, and fulfill roles of authority.

nodes.name: 需求与欲望
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.1.K-2. Explain how scarcity necessitates decision making.
D2.Eco.2.K-2. Identify the benefits and costs of making various personal decisions.

nodes.name: 货币与工作
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.3.K-2. Describe the skills and knowledge required to produce certain goods and services.
D2.Eco.4.K-2. Describe the goods and services that people produce and consume.

nodes.name: 供给与需求
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.6.6-8. Explain how changes in supply and demand cause changes in prices and quantities of goods and services, labor, credit, and foreign currencies.

nodes.name: 全球经济
curriculum_points: The study of economics provides students with the concepts and tools necessary for an economic way of thinking and helps students understand the interaction of buyers and sellers in markets, workings of the national economy, and interactions within the global marketplace.

nodes.name: 地图与地球仪
curriculum_points: Students will distinguish between maps and globes, using cardinal directions and map keys to locate physical and human features, demonstrating an understanding of the purpose and utility of each representation.

nodes.name: 美国各区域
curriculum_points: Students will identify and compare the defining human and physical characteristics of major regions in the United States (e.g., Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, Southwest, West), analyzing how these characteristics influence community life and economic activities.

nodes.name: 世界地理
curriculum_points: Students will locate and identify the continents and major oceans on a world map or globe, and describe basic physical and human characteristics of diverse global communities, fostering a foundational sense of global interconnectedness.

nodes.name: 人地关系
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.3.3-5. Identify examples of the variety of resources (human capital, physical capital, and natural resources) that are used to produce goods and services.

nodes.name: 家庭与社区史
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.14.K-2. Describe how people have tried to improve their communities over time.

nodes.name: 美国历史
curriculum_points: Civics requires other knowledge too; students should also learn about state and local governments; markets; courts and legal systems; civil society; other nations’ systems and practices; international institutions; and the techniques available to citizens for preserving and changing a society.

nodes.name: 世界历史
curriculum_points: Civics requires other knowledge too; students should also learn about state and local governments; markets; courts and legal systems; civil society; other nations’ systems and practices; international institutions; and the techniques available to citizens for preserving and changing a society.

nodes.name: 一手资料
curriculum_points: Learning these virtues and principles requires obtaining factual knowledge of written provisions found in important texts such as the founding documents of the United States. It also means coming to understand diverse arguments that have been made about these documents and their meanings.
